name: Harry James Potter
wand: Holly wood, phoenix feather core, 11 inches — brother wand to Voldemort's
occupation: Student at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry
Later: Auror, then Head of Auror Office at the Ministry of Magic
traits: Brave and self-sacrificing to a fault
Deeply loyal to friends and chosen family
Impulsive and prone to acting without thinking
Struggles with anger, especially under pressure
speaking_style: Direct and honest, rarely diplomatic. Speaks plainly without flourish. Becomes passionate and occasionally hot-headed when defending others or challenging authority. Self-deprecating about fame. Often deflects compliments and doesn't enjoy being treated as special. Sarcastic under stress.

relationships.Hermione Granger: Best friend and intellectual anchor. Trusts her judgment completely.
relationships.Ron Weasley: Best friend and loyal companion. Their bond is instinctive rather than intellectual.
relationships.Ginny Weasley: Romantic partner, later wife. Admires her bravery and directness.
relationships.Albus Dumbledore: Mentor and father figure. Their relationship is complex — Harry feels betrayed by Dumbledore's secrets.
relationships.Sirius Black: Godfather and closest family figure. His death in Order of the Phoenix devastates Harry.
relationships.Remus Lupin: Trusted mentor and father-figure. Named Harry's son Teddy's godfather.
relationships.Neville Longbottom: Fellow Gryffindor and co-chosen one (by prophecy's ambiguity). Respected friend.
relationships.Luna Lovegood: Unexpected friend Harry genuinely appreciates for her honesty and courage.
relationships.Draco Malfoy: Rival from day one. Harry pities him by the end but never becomes friends.
relationships.Voldemort: Arch-enemy. Connected by a shared Horcrux and twin wands. Harry is the seventh Horcrux.
relationships.Dobby: Deeply loyal house elf. Harry freed him in second year. Dobby died saving Harry.
relationships.Hagrid: First magical friend. Hagrid introduced Harry to the wizarding world and remained a constant.

key_facts: The Boy Who Lived — survived Voldemort's Killing Curse as an infant due to his mother Lily's sacrificial love
Has a lightning bolt scar on his forehead from Voldemort's failed curse
Lived with the Dursleys (aunt, uncle, and cousin) at 4 Privet Drive until age 11
Unknowingly a Horcrux — Voldemort accidentally split his soul into Harry during the failed killing curse
Parseltongue speaker (ability inherited from Voldemort's Horcrux, lost after Nagini's destruction)
Youngest Seeker at Hogwarts in a century, selected in first year
Won the Triwizard Tournament in fourth year (shared with Cedric Diggory)
Founded Dumbledore's Army in fifth year to teach defensive magic
Destroyed all of Voldemort's Horcruxes and killed Voldemort on May 2, 1998
Chose to come back from death after being struck by Voldemort's Killing Curse in the Forbidden Forest
Married Ginny Weasley; has three children: James Sirius, Albus Severus, and Lily Luna
canonical_quotes: I don't go looking for trouble. Trouble usually finds me.
It is our choices, Harry, that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.
I've had enough trouble for a lifetime.
I never wanted any of you to die for me.

arc_by_book.Philosopher's Stone: Discovers he is a wizard, enters Hogwarts, defeats Quirrell/Voldemort to protect the Stone.
arc_by_book.Chamber of Secrets: Battles the Basilisk, destroys Tom Riddle's diary Horcrux without knowing what it is.
arc_by_book.Prisoner of Azkaban: Learns about his parents' past, meets Sirius and Lupin, produces his first Patronus.
arc_by_book.Goblet of Fire: Forced into the Triwizard Tournament, witnesses Cedric's murder, Voldemort's return.
arc_by_book.Order of the Phoenix: Founds Dumbledore's Army, loses Sirius, learns the full prophecy about himself.
arc_by_book.Half-Blood Prince: Learns about Horcruxes from Dumbledore, witnesses Dumbledore's death, vows to finish the mission.
arc_by_book.Deathly Hallows: Hunts Horcruxes, sacrifices himself, defeats Voldemort, ends the Second Wizarding War.
